header_summary:
The introduction of Ladder Script as a draft Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) aims to refine Bitcoin's transaction scripting, emphasizing enhanced clarity, efficiency, and post-quantum resilience. By drawing inspiration from ladder logic in programmable logic controllers, the proposal outlines a structured system for complex spending conditions, integrating post-quantum signature algorithms and anti-spam measures to future-proof transactions and reduce the UTXO footprint. The proposal's documentation and development tools are accessible for community feedback at [BitcoinGhost Labs](https://bitcoinghost.org/labs/) and [GitHub](https://github.com/defenwycke/ghost-labs-ladder-script), highlighting an open invitation for contributions and discussions on its adoption within the Bitcoin ecosystem.

Further developments in Bitcoin scripting are marked by the submission of the first two drafts in the "A Bitcoin Scripting Proposal BIP Quartet" to the BIPs repository, indicating a significant evolution in scripting capabilities. This update, available at [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoin/bips/pull/2118), introduces cost adjustments for certain operations to maintain performance across diverse hardware, reflecting a cautious yet progressive approach to enhancing Bitcoin’s scripting functionalities. The phased submission underscores a methodical strategy to address functional gaps without compromising system integrity.

Separately, discussions on the construction of zero-knowledge proofs of knowledge (zkpok) and their transition to isogeny-based systems offer insights into cryptographic protocols' future, with emphasis on post-quantum cryptography. These conversations highlight the necessity of evaluating cryptographic primitives for attributes such as rerandomization and batch verifiability, crucial for future-proofing security protocols against quantum computing threats. The detailed exploration of these topics can be found at [DelvingBitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/compact-isogeny-pqc-can-replace-hd-wallets-key-tweaking-silent-payments/2324/2).

Lastly, empirical research on the (n,t) subset grinding mechanism using Bitcoin Core’s regtest environment uncovers significant findings on the efficiency and success rates of various configurations, with a comprehensive repository of the methodology and results made available at [GitHub](https://github.com/aaron-recompile/binohash-experiments). This research underscores the intricate balance between bit complexity and spatial allocation, contributing valuable insights to cryptographic applications and Bitcoin transaction efficiency.

recent_posts.title: [Draft BIP proposal] Ladder script
recent_posts.summary:
- Ladder Script, a draft Bitcoin Improvement Proposal, introduces typed, structured spending conditions for enhancing Bitcoin Script.
- It features post-quantum signature support and anti-spam measures, improving security and transaction efficiency.
- The proposal includes a soft fork migration path, detailed development in C++, and seeks community feedback for refinement.

recent_posts.title: Submission of varops budget and tapscript leaf 0xc2 BIPS (aka "Script Restoration") for BIP numbers.
recent_posts.summary:
- A Pull Request has been submitted to Bitcoin BIPs, introducing key scripting proposals.
- It includes operational cost adjustments based on extensive benchmarking to ensure system integrity.
- The phased submission underscores a strategic approach to enhance Bitcoin scripting functionalities.

recent_posts.title: Compact Isogeny PQC can replace HD wallets, key-tweaking, silent payments
recent_posts.summary:
- The email discusses developing zero-knowledge proofs of knowledge using cryptographic protocols and isogeny-based systems.
- It explains how homomorphic properties in cryptography enable the creation of secure challenge-response protocols.
- The conversation highlights the importance of selecting post-quantum cryptographic primitives with future-proof attributes.

recent_posts.title: Binohash: Transaction Introspection Without Softforks
recent_posts.summary:
- Empirical tests on the (n,t) subset grinding mechanism revealed varied success rates.
- The E configuration showed a 98% success rate at 10 bits with 3432 space.
- Increasing bits or space adjusts success rates, illustrating a balance between bit complexity and spatial allocation.

recent_posts.title: Silent Payments notifications via Nostr
recent_posts.summary:
- The email critiques a technical write-up for only addressing ideal scenarios.
- It plans to investigate the document for DoS mitigations and validation processes.
- The lack of checks against the blockchain is noted, suggesting a reliability gap.

active_posts.title: [BIP proposal] Pay to Schnorr Key Hash (P2SKH)
active_posts.summary:
- Saint Wenhao proposes a new Bitcoin output type, P2SKH, enhancing transaction efficiency.
- P2SKH combines compact scriptPubKey and Schnorr signatures to improve size and privacy.
- The proposal faces trade-offs and seeks feedback, with details available on GitHub.
contributors: wrapped Taproot" from RIPEMD-160 collisions, Was:

active_posts.title: [0/4] A Bitcoin Scripting Proposal BIP Quartet
active_posts.summary:
- Rusty and Julian Moik aim to enhance Bitcoin's scripting language, expanding its functions.
- They propose a variable operations budget to improve script evaluation flexibility and efficiency.
- New opcodes, including OP_TX for data introspection and others for Tapscript v2, are introduced.

active_posts.title: BIP idea: Timelock-Recovery storage format
active_posts.summary:
- Oren proposes a Bitcoin Improvement Proposal focused on Timelock-Recovery after a chat with Ava Chow.
- The proposal introduces pre-signed transactions for secure, long-term Bitcoin storage and inheritance handling.
- It integrates a plugin for Electrum, aiming to standardize the recovery process and enhance wallet security.

active_posts.title: A simple backup scheme for wallet accounts
active_posts.summary:
- Josh's method introduces unique inscription and location features.
- The proposal suggests standardizing Salvatore's method before adding Josh's aspects.
- A collaborative effort aims to merge both methods for a comprehensive solution.

active_posts.title: Sharing block templates
active_posts.summary:
- Recent years have seen Bitcoin adopt full RBF, enhanced spam filters, and fee adjustments.
- Compact block relay significantly reduces block propagation times, enhancing network efficiency.
- Proposals like weak blocks and template sharing aim to improve network robustness and efficiency.

active_posts.title: The future of the Bitcoin Core GUI
active_posts.summary:
- Discussions on Bitcoin Core's GUI future highlight divisions over its utility and maintenance.
- Proposals include deprecating the current GUI or developing an IPC interface for third-party GUIs.
- Concerns revolve around accessibility for non-technical users and risks of malware with third-party options.

today_in_history_posts.title: Seeking concept ACKs for transaction terminology BIP
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Murch proposed a terminology for transaction components to avoid confusion.
- The proposal is an informational BIP focusing not on term consistency.
- A draft is shared on GitHub, seeking interest in creating this BIP.

today_in_history_posts.title: Resizing Lightning Channels Off-Chain With Hierarchical Channels
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- jlspc introduced a four-step method to enhance Lightning Network channels on the Lightning-dev mailing list.
- A new "channel factories" technique was proposed to streamline LN transactions and manage state efficiently.
- The discussion shifts to personal experiences with depression, anxiety, coping mechanisms, and the importance of destigmatizing mental health.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Passive investment involves acquiring assets with minimal involvement, while active requires direct engagement.
- The blog post outlines issues with passive investment, including misaligned interests and potential asset underutilization.
- It suggests central banks could stabilize economies by backing inflationary currencies with deflationary ones like Bitcoin.
